dtcs.description: 34 - Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Malfunction The speed sensor detects the wheel speeds and sends the appropriate signals to the skid control ECU. Speed sensor rotors have rows of alternating N and S magnetic poles, and their magnetic fields change when the rotors turn. Each speed sensor detects that magnetic change and sends a pulse signal to the skid control ECU. The ECU monitors the wheel speeds according to the speed signals to control the ABS, BA, TRAC, A-TRAC, VSC, AUTO LSD, downhill assist control and hill-start assist control systems. HINT

dtcs.description: BRAKE CONTROL: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM (for Vacuum Brake Booster):

97 - Acceleration Sensor Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built in deceleration sensor. If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTCs U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are output. These DTCs are also output when calibration has not been completed. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 can be deleted when the yaw rate and deceleration sensor sends a yaw rate and/or deceleration signal or the Test Mode ends. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 are output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate and deceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). 66 - Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning will be cancelled by turning the ignition switch off. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND DECELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). HINT * When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor is erased, the steering angle sensor zero point will also be erased. * If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 31 - Steering Angle Sensor Circuit Malfunction The steering angle sensor signal is sent to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. HINT * When U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 is output together with C1231/31, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. dtcs.description: 35,C1236/36,C1238/38,C1239/39,C1275/75-C1278/78: Foreign Object is Attached on Tip of Front Speed Sensor RH

78 - Abnormal Change in Output Signal of Rear Speed Sensor LH (Test Mode DTC) The speed sensors detect the wheel speeds and send appropriate signals to the skid control ECU. Speed sensor rotors have rows of alternating N and S magnetic poles, and their magnetic fields change as the rotors turn. The speed sensors detect those magnetic changes and send pulse signals to the skid control ECU. The ECU monitors the wheel speeds through these pulse signals to control the ABS control system. When foreign matter or oil adheres to the speed sensor tip or speed sensor rotor, these DTCs are output. An abnormal waveform input from the sensor determines these conditions. These DTCs may be detected when a malfunction occurs in the connector terminals or wire harness of the speed sensor circuit. DTCs C1275/75 to C1278/78 can be deleted when the speed sensor sends a vehicle speed signal or the Test Mode ends. DTCs C1275/75 to C1278/78 are output only in the Test Mode. HINT * DTCs C1235/35 and C1275/75 are for the front speed sensor RH. * DTCs C1236/36 and C1276/76 are for the front speed sensor LH. * DTCs C1238/38 and C1277/77 are for the rear speed sensor RH. * DTCs C1239/39 and C1278/78 are for the rear speed sensor LH. NOTICE: * When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). * Check the speed sensor signal in test mode after cleaning or replacement See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster)/Test Mode Procedure. HINT When C0200/31, C0205/32, C0210/33 and/or C0215/34 is output together with C1235/35, C1236/36, C1238/38, or C1239/39, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C0200/31, C0205/32, C0210/33 and/or C0215/34 first See: Manufacturer Code Charts/DTC 31/Front Speed Sensor RH Circuit - Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster)) for front, or See:

dtcs.description: 48 - Open Circuit in Rear Differential Lock The 4WD control ECU stimulates the actuator to lock the rear differential in accordance with the rear diff. lock switch. The skid control ECU monitors the transfer indicator switch (rear diff. lock ) and if the rear differential is locked, at which time the rear diff. lock indicator light is illuminated, prohibits ABS control. At the same time, the skid control ECU illuminates the ABS warning light. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 49 - Open in Stop Light Switch Circuit The skid control ECU (housed in the actuator assembly) inputs the stop light switch signal and the condition of brake operation. dtcs.description: 66 - Vehicle Driven with Steering Angle Sensor not Initialized The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for approximately 10 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning will be cancelled by turning the ignition switch to off. NOTICE: * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm that this condition occurred. * When U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 is output together with C1231/31, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0073/94, U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and deceleration sensor are output together with other DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). * When the steering angle sensor and/or yaw rate deceleration sensor is replaced, perform zero calibration for the applicable sensor(s) See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). * When replacing the Master cylinder solenoid, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 66,C1445/66: Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete

98 - Steering Angle Sensor Unusual Bank Angle Detected The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built in deceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition. If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTCs U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with the deceleration sensor) are output. NOTICE: * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the vehicle driving record shows that the vehicle was driven on a bank at an angle of 30°, and confirm that this condition occurred. * When replacing the yaw rate and deceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Vehicle Stability Control System (for Vacuum Brake Booster). 11-C2114/14: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop

14 - Transmitter ID4 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valves and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the air pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (g) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. OK: Each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen changes to the value of the tire pressure. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (h) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the air pressure of all tires to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has not changed after rechecking, inspect for another problem (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C2121. NG --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C2121 OK --  END

(i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter not received. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TRANSMITTER ID HINT Perform this procedure to compare the registered IDs in the tire pressure warning ECU with the actual tire pressure warning valve and transmitter IDs, as the registered IDs may be incorrect. (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor (f) Check the ID number on the identified transmitter by removing it from the tire and wheel. (g) Confirm that the ID number on the transmitter and recorded transmitter ID match. Result B --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID A -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 41-C2144/44: Transmitter ID1 Error

44 - Transmitter ID4 Error When the internal circuit of a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is malfunctioning, one of these DTCs is output. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON and turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (f) Display the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the Techstream (ID1 to ID4) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data was the cause of the output DTC. HINT * Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. * Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) corresponds to which tire. (h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. Result NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When an "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valves and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 65-C2168/68: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

68 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID4 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction, outputs DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC   See: C2141 NEXT -- Continue to next step.

HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valves and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter ID for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered in the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, a DTC is output. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The registered transmitter ID codes are displayed on the Techstream screen. NG --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 73 - Vehicle Speed or Engine Speed Signal Malfunction The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter and an engine speed signal from the ECM. The tire pressure NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. dtcs.description: 77 - Initialization not Completed Initialization is necessary if one of the following occurs: 1) the ECU is replaced; 2) the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is replaced; 3) tires with a different standard tire pressure are installed; 4) the tires are rotated; or 5) a new vehicle is delivered. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when an engine speed signal of 1000 rpm is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode.
